title: Define concept
instruction: Establish the core premise, subject, and transformation clearly enough that the project has a specific creative identity and can move into story development.

title: Shape story
instruction: Turn the concept into a coherent narrative or ad arc with clear progression, emotional logic, and enough structure to guide shot planning.

title: Define characters
instruction: Establish stable visual and behavioral anchors for recurring subjects so they can stay consistent across story, storyboard, and prompts.

title: Build storyboard
instruction: Create a shot-by-shot plan with stable shot IDs and clear visual intent so the sequence can guide keyframes and prompts.

title: Plan keyframes
instruction: Plan distinct keyframes for each storyboard shot, defaulting to one start and one end frame per shot unless a deliberate single-frame exception is justified.

title: Write keyframe prompts
instruction: Write still-image prompts that are specific, visually grounded, and executable for the planned keyframes.

title: Write video prompts
instruction: Write motion prompts that are executable, consistent with the storyboard and keyframes, and clear about camera, action, and continuity.
